topic.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L) & Carrier Integration

learningObjectives.title: Understand BN4L role
learningObjectives.description: Explain how BN4L extends SAP TM for external logistics collaboration.

learningObjectives.title: Understand carrier collaboration flow
learningObjectives.description: Describe tendering, acceptance, tracking, POD, and invoice exchange steps.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate TM and network capabilities
learningObjectives.description: Clarify internal transportation management versus partner communication.

sections.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics & Carrier Integration

sections.content: SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L), formerly SAP Logistics Business Network (LBN), extends SAP Transportation Management by connecting shippers, carriers, freight forwarders and logistics partners through a cloud-based collaboration platform. It enables electronic tendering, shipment collaboration, real-time tracking and document exchange.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP TM manages transportation internally, while SAP Business Network for Logistics enables collaboration with external logistics partners.

A manufacturer creates a Freight Order in SAP TM. The shipment is electronically tendered to multiple carriers through SAP Business Network for Logistics. One carrier accepts the tender, shares GPS updates during transportation and submits Proof of Delivery and invoice electronically after delivery.

sections.title: SAP TM vs SAP Business Network for Logistics
Best Practice
sections.content: Integrate key carriers with SAP Business Network for Logistics to automate tendering, shipment tracking and document exchange instead of relying on manual communication.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational consumer goods company connected over 250 logistics providers through SAP Business Network for Logistics. Electronic tendering reduced carrier response time by more than 60%, while shipment visibility improved significantly.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: For SAP TM certification, remember that SAP Business Network for Logistics primarily supports carrier collaboration, freight tendering, shipment tracking and document exchange.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: SAP Business Network for Logistics does not replace SAP TM. It extends SAP TM by providing collaboration with external logistics partners.

items: SAP Business Network for Logistics connects shippers and carriers.
It supports electronic freight tendering and carrier collaboration.
Real-time shipment updates are shared through the network.
Proof of Delivery and invoices can be exchanged electronically.
Integration improves transportation visibility and logistics efficiency.
